ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code where the city is located */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the city */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the city in meters */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the city */,
  "climate_type" TEXT /* Climate type of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city for which the report is made */,
  "temp_max" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded */,
  "temp_min" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity level in percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the wind (e.g., N, NE, E) */,
  "cloudiness" INTEGER /* Cloudiness percentage */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the report */,
  "source" TEXT /* Identifier for the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_records" (
  "temp_record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature record */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city related to the temperature */,
  "temp_max" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded */,
  "temp_min" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the temperature record */,
  "source" TEXT /* Identifier for the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temp_record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_records_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "humidity_records" (
  "humidity_record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each humidity record */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city related to the humidity */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity level in percentage */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the humidity record */,
  "source" TEXT /* Identifier for the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("humidity_record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_humidity_records_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wind_speed_records" (
  "wind_record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wind speed record */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city related to the wind speed */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the wind (e.g., N, NE, E) */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the wind speed record */,
  "source" TEXT /* Identifier for the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wind_record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wind_speed_records_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cloudiness_records" (
  "cloudiness_record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cloudiness record */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city related to the cloudiness */,
  "cloudiness" INTEGER /* Cloudiness percentage */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the cloudiness record */,
  "source" TEXT /* Identifier for the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cloudiness_record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cloudiness_records_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_access_logs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES weather_reports ("report_id")
)
